segments.text: PART 4
For my presentation today, I'm going to talk about the Céide Fields in the northwest of Ireland, one of the largest Neolithic sites in the world.
segments.translation: 在今天的演讲中，我将介绍爱尔兰西北部的塞伊德田野，这是世界上最大的新石器时代遗址之一。

segments.text: I recently visited this site and observed the work that is currently being done by a team of archaeologists there.
segments.translation: 我最近参观了这个遗址，并观察了考古队目前正在那里进行的工作。

segments.text: The site was first discovered in the 1930s by a local teacher, Patrick Caulfield.20
segments.translation: 世纪30年代，当地教师帕特里克·考尔菲尔德(Patrick Caulfield)首次发现了这个遗址。

segments.text: He noticed that when local people were digging in the bog, they were constantly hitting against what seemed to be rows of stones.
segments.translation: 他注意到，当地人在沼泽地里挖掘时，会不断撞击到一排排石头。

segments.text: He realised that these must be walls and that they must be thousands of years old for them to predate the bog which subsequently grew over them.
segments.translation: 他意识到这些石头一定是城墙，而且有数千年的历史，在沼泽覆盖这片土地前就已经存在了。

segments.text: He wrote to the National Museum in Dublin to ask them to investigate, but no one took him seriously.
segments.translation: 他写信给都柏林的国家博物馆，要求他们进行调查，但没有人把他的话当回事。

segments.text: It wasn't until 40 years later, when Patrick Caulfield's son Seamus, who had become an archaeologist by then, began to explore further.
segments.translation: 直到40年后，帕特里克·考尔菲尔德的儿子谢默斯(Seamus)才开始进一步探索，当时他已经成为一名考古学家。

segments.text: He inserted iron probes into the bog to map the formation of the stones, a traditional method which local people had always used for finding fuel buried in the bog for thousands of years.
segments.translation: 他在沼泽中插入铁探针，用以绘制石头的形成图，这是当地人几千年来一直使用的传统方法，以寻找埋在沼泽中的燃料。

segments.text: Carbon dating later proved that the site was over 5,000 years old and was the largest Neolithic site in Ireland.
segments.translation: 碳年代测定法后来证明，该遗址距今已有五千多年的历史，是爱尔兰最大的新石器时代遗址。

segments.text: Thanks to the bog which covers the area, the remains of the settlement at Céide Fields, which is over 5,000 years old, are extremely well-preserved.
segments.translation: 由于该遗址被沼泽覆盖，赛德菲尔德定居点遗址保存的非常好，这些遗址有五千多年的历史了。

segments.text: A bog is 90 percent water; its soil is so saturated that when the grasses and heathers that grow on its surface die, they don't fully decay but accumulate in layers.
segments.translation: 沼泽 90% 都是水；它的土壤水份非常饱和，以至于生长在其表面的草和红叶石楠死后不会完全腐烂，而是层层堆积。

segments.text: Objects remain so well preserved in these conditions because of the acidity of the peat and the deficiency of oxygen.
segments.translation: 由于泥炭的酸性和缺氧，物品在这种条件下依然保存得很好。

segments.text: At least 175 days of rain a year are required for this to happen; this part of Ireland gets an average of 225 days.
segments.translation: 要达到这样的条件，每年至少要有175天的降雨；而爱尔兰这一地区的平均降雨天数为 225 天。

segments.text: The Neolithic farmers at Céide would have enjoyed several centuries of relative peace and stability.
segments.translation: 在新石器时代，赛伊德地区的农民享受了几个世纪的和平稳定。

segments.text: Neolithic farmers generally lived in larger communities than their predecessors, with a number of houses built around a community building.
segments.translation: 与前人相比，新石器时代的农民一般居住在更大的社区中，在社区建筑周围建造了许多房子。

segments.text: As they lived in permanent settlements, Neolithic farmers were able to build bigger houses.
segments.translation: 他们住在永久性定居点,新石器时代的农民能够建立更大的房子。

segments.text: These weren't round as people often assume, but rectangular with a small hole in the roof that allowed smoke to escape.
segments.translation: 这些房子并不像人们通常认为的那样是圆形的，而是长方形的，屋顶上有一个小孔，可以让烟雾排出。

segments.text: This is one of many innovations and indicates that the Neolithic farmers were the first people to cook indoors.
segments.translation: 这是众多创新之一，表明新石器时代的农民是最早在室内做饭的人。

segments.text: Another new technology that Neolithic settlers brought to Ireland was pottery. Fragments of Neolithic pots have been found in Céide and elsewhere in Ireland.
segments.translation: 新石器时代移民带到爱尔兰的另一项新技术是制陶。在塞伊德和爱尔兰其他地方都发现了新石器时代的陶器碎片。

segments.text: The pots were used for many things; as well as for storing food, pots were filled with a small amount of fat and when this was set alight, they served as lamps.
segments.translation: 罐子的用途很多。除了用来储存食物，罐子里还装有少量脂肪，点燃后就成了灯。

segments.text: It's thought that the Céide Fields were mainly used as paddocks for animals to graze in.
segments.translation: 人们认为，塞伊德田野主要被用作牲畜吃草的围场。

segments.text: Evidence from the Céide Fields suggests that each plot of land was of a suitable size to sustain an extended family.
segments.translation: 塞伊德田野的证据表明，每块土地的面积都适合维持一个大家庭的生活。

segments.text: They may have used a system of rotational grazing in order to prevent over-grazing and to allow for plant recovery and regrowth.
segments.translation: 为防止过度放牧，他们采用了轮牧制度以防止过度放牧，使得植物得以恢复和重新生长。

segments.text: This must have been a year-round activity as no structures have been found which would have been used to shelter animals in the winter.
segments.translation: 这必定是一项全年都在进行的活动，因为目前还没有发现在冬季用于动物栖息的建筑。

segments.text: However, archaeologists believe that this way of life at Céide ceased abruptly. Why was this?
segments.translation: 然而，考古学家认为，塞伊德的这种生活方式突然停止了。这是为什么呢？

segments.text: Well, several factors may have contributed to the changing circumstances.
segments.translation: 有几个因素可能导致了环境的变化。

segments.text: The soil would have become less productive and led to the abandonment of farming.
segments.translation: 土壤的生产力会降低，导致人们放弃耕作。

segments.text: The crop rotation system was partly responsible for this as it would have been very intensive and was not sustainable.
segments.translation: 轮种制度是造成这种情况的部分原因，因为轮种会非常密集，无法持续。

segments.text: But there were also climatic pressures too.
segments.translation: 不过，气候也是原因之一。

segments.text: The farmers at Céide would have enjoyed a relatively dry period, but this began to change and the conditions became wetter as there was a lot more rain.
segments.translation: 塞伊德的农民本来可以享受一段相对干燥的时期，但情况开始发生变化，随着雨水增多，环境变得更加潮湿。

segments.text: It was these conditions that encouraged the bog to form over the area which survives today.
segments.translation: 正是这些条件促使沼泽在这一地区形成，并延续至今。

segments.text: So now I'd like to show you some…
segments.translation: 现在我想给你们看一些……
